mechanism: Inhibits FLT3-ITD and multiple kinases, suppressing AML cell proliferation
additional_notes: Also known as PKC412; approved for FLT3-mutant AML; MOLM-13 (FLT3-ITD) is highly sensitive

mechanism: Selectively inhibits FLT3 and AXL kinases, blocking FLT3-ITD signaling
additional_notes: Also known as ASP2215; approved for relapsed/refractory FLT3-mutant AML

mechanism: Potent type II FLT3 inhibitor, blocking FLT3-ITD-driven signaling
additional_notes: Also known as AC220; widely used in MOLM-13 and MV4-11 models

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and multiple RAF and receptor tyrosine kinases, reducing AML cell growth
additional_notes: Approved for solid tumors; used off-label and in trials for FLT3-ITD AML

mechanism: Inhibits SRC family kinases, ABL and related targets, impairing survival signaling
additional_notes: Approved for CML/ALL; shows anti-proliferative activity in AML cell lines

mechanism: Selective BCL-2 inhibitor inducing mitochondrial apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as ABT-199; approved for AML in combination regimens; synergizes with FLT3 inhibitors

mechanism: Incorporates into DNA/RNA and inhibits DNA methyltransferase, causing hypomethylation and cytotoxicity
additional_notes: Standard of care for MDS/AML; effects are time-dependent, often requiring 72–96 h exposure

mechanism: Inhibits DNA methyltransferase after incorporation into DNA, leading to hypomethylation and growth inhibition
additional_notes: Approved for AML/MDS; time-dependent epigenetic effects (72–96 h exposure recommended)

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases, increasing histone acetylation and inducing apoptosis
additional_notes: Also known as SAHA; approved for cutaneous T-cell lymphoma; broad anti-leukemic activity

mechanism: Potent pan-histone deacetylase inhibitor, promoting apoptosis and cell cycle arrest
additional_notes: Approved for multiple myeloma; AML proliferation IC50 often 5–50 nM; focus 5–500 nM within this range

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK2, suppressing JAK/STAT signaling implicated in some AML subsets
additional_notes: Approved for myelofibrosis, polycythemia vera, and GVHD; modest direct cytotoxicity but useful in combinations

mechanism: Inhibits BTK and off-target TEC/SRC family kinases, impacting survival signaling
additional_notes: Approved for multiple B-cell malignancies; shows AML growth inhibition in certain subtypes

mechanism: Inhibits BCR-ABL, PDGFR, and c-KIT tyrosine kinases, reducing signaling in susceptible AML subsets
additional_notes: Approved for CML, GIST and others; some AMLs with c-KIT or PDGFR alterations show partial response

category: AMPK activator / mitochondrial complex I inhibitor
mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial complex I, activates AMPK, and downregulates mTOR signaling
additional_notes: Widely used antidiabetic drug; in vitro AML IC50 is high µM–low mM (pharmacologic, not physiologic range)

mechanism: Inhibits ALDH and, in presence of copper, forms complexes that disrupt proteasome and redox homeostasis
additional_notes: Anti-alcoholism drug; activity against ALDH-positive leukemic stem/progenitor cells; often tested with CuCl2 (0.5–2 µM)

mechanism: Raises lysosomal pH and blocks autophagic flux, impairing AML cell survival under stress
additional_notes: Antimalarial; hydroxychloroquine is a related alternative; both used as autophagy modulators in cancer trials

mechanism: Induces degradation of PML-RARα (in APL) and generates oxidative and mitochondrial stress
additional_notes: Also known as ATO; approved for APL; has some activity in non-APL AML at higher doses

mechanism: Binds cereblon E3 ligase complex, altering substrate degradation and immune modulation
additional_notes: IMiD; context-dependent direct AML cytotoxicity; lenalidomide is a more potent related IMiD used in MDS/MM

mechanism: Inhibits mTORC1, reducing protein synthesis and cell growth signaling
additional_notes: Also known as RAD001; AML IC50 typically 1–100 nM for signaling and 10 nM–1 µM for viability; focus 5–500 nM in this range

mechanism: Reversibly inhibits the 26S proteasome, leading to accumulation of misfolded proteins and apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved for multiple myeloma and mantle cell lymphoma; AML IC50 often 2–50 nM; focus 2–200 nM within this range
